assumptions: A browser, an accessible email inbox, and CodePen are available.
No ArcGIS account, subscription, organization, portal, API key, authentication choice, or client SDK is assumed.

developer_goal: Follow Esri's general developer getting-started guidance to create credentials and display the first documented map.

first_success.outcome: In the JavaScript candidate, the CodePen application displays the topographic basemap for the Santa Monica Mountains and allows pan and zoom.
first_success.observable_signal: Running the pen displays the topographic basemap centered near `-118.805, 34.020` at zoom 13 with the zoom component.

route_selection.surface: ArcGIS Location Platform API key and JavaScript Display a map
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: ArcGIS Location Platform API key and JavaScript Display a map
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: ArcGIS Online account route
candidates.reason: ArcGIS Online is a peer account type with a multi-user organization, organization-admin controls, and different billing ownership.

candidates.name: OAuth 2.0 user authentication
candidates.reason: It is a peer authentication route for private applications and changes credential creation, user consent, and billing ownership.

candidates.name: Other Maps SDKs and open-source clients
candidates.reason: The Display a map tutorial is offered for multiple web and native SDKs without a universal client default.

required_path.action: Complete Esri Developer account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Esri Developer account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Esri Developer candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: Running the pen displays the topographic basemap centered near `-118.805, 34.020` at zoom 13 with the zoom component.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Choose an eligible ArcGIS account route; the candidate uses a free ArcGIS Location Platform account and its single-user organization.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'For the email candidate, provide a unique email, compliant unique username and password, unique 3-to-16-character portal URL, and security question and answer, then accept the platform agreement and privacy policy.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'verification', 'requirement': 'Activate the account from the confirmation email within 24 hours.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: Account, authentication, and client choices materially change the route and no universal combination is selected.
Email signup requires unique account and portal identifiers, a security answer, legal acceptance, and email activation.
The key must have the Basemap styles service privilege.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Display a map with the ArcGIS Basemap Styles service.

excluded.item: Additional map layers, feature styling, geocoding, routing, data hosting, production deployment, license review, usage monitoring, billing configuration, API-key rotation, and OAuth implementation occ
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Get started | Documentation | Esri Developer
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: Account management | ArcGIS Location Platform
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Account management | ArcGIS Location Platform

evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Tutorial: Create an API key | Documentation | Esri Developer

uncertainties.question: Which account, authentication method, and client should define Esri Developer's company-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes organization requirements, billing ownership, credential and consent work, runtime setup, and code.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The general page presents these as purpose-dependent peers; the candidate uses first-listed options rather than an official universal default.

uncertainties.question: Can a brand-new Location Platform user currently sign up with a social identity?
uncertainties.impact: The new-user identity path and verification steps differ.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The account page first says social signup is supported, but its Social media section says new social signup is not currently supported and limits it to migrated pre-June-2024 accounts.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Esri Developer show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
